idea_summary: The signal looks for short-term price acceleration that may persist across liquid names.
rationale: The hypothesis is that recent price acceleration can capture delayed reaction in liquid instruments.
template_notes: This is a reusable short-horizon continuation template rather than a one-off expression tuned to a single dataset.
stability_notes: Checked on TOP3000 and TOP1000 and compared 2021-2024 versus 2024-2025 slices.
cross_universe_notes: The signal is stable on TOP3000 and TOP1000 but degrades on TOP500, so the broader liquid universe is intentional.
cross_region_notes: Tried in USA and EUR; USA is materially stronger, so the region choice is deliberate rather than cherry-picked after many failures.
coverage_notes: Uses price-volume data with full coverage and no special backfill requirement.
turnover_notes: Turnover stays below the current pool median and the highest-turnover names were reviewed before considering submission.
diversification_notes: The signal is intended to add a short-horizon price-volume component to a pool dominated by slower model/fundamental signals.
capacity_notes: Turnover and liquidity profile were reviewed before considering submission.
liquidity_notes: The expression is only considered in liquid universes and does not rely on sparse fields or aggressive backfill.
portfolio_fit_notes: Correlation to the currently submitted short-horizon price signals is lower than the pool median, so it fills a real gap.
submission_priority_notes: This is the strongest candidate in today's batch; two close variants were withheld because they added little incremental diversification.
value_factor_notes: Expected to help value factor through lower prod correlation and a regular signal source not yet present in the current submitted set.
